meta.title: Eder Queiroz — Software Engineer
meta.description: Eder Queiroz — software engineer. Reliable, scalable backends with Node.js, NestJS and AWS. See the systems I built, animated live.
hero.headline: I build what stays invisible when it works.
hero.support: Notifications that always arrive. Balances never redeemed twice. Queues that take the load.
about.title: I'm drawn to the problems that only show up when something breaks.
about.body: People call me Edim. I'm a full-stack developer at Grão Direto — concurrency, guaranteed delivery and consistency under load are my idea of fun. I finish my Computer Engineering degree at IFTM in 2026 and keep one foot in research. To me, a good day is when the system takes the pressure and nobody notices.
skills.title: The tools — and where each one went into battle.
skills.hint: Hover or tap each one to see the real case.
skills.node.tip: The backbone of the backend at Grão Direto — the services that power the platform.
skills.nest.tip: The framework of the services I maintain: tidy modules, queues and transactions.
skills.aws.tip: Where the systems run. This is where I use SQS, FIFO queues and DLQs — see the queue case.
skills.sqs.tip: The FIFO queue that killed Twilio rate-limit errors and took delivery to 100%.
skills.flutter.tip: Interactive trading timeline and dashboards, live in Grão Direto's app.
skills.dart.tip: My everyday mobile language, alongside Flutter.
skills.react.tip: The web platform's front-end, including an A/B-tested signup (+12% conversion).
skills.postgres.tip: Where the points ledger lives — pessimistic locks and transactions that guarantee consistency.
cases.title: Three real problems, animated live.
cases.subtitle: Each one is a problem I solved in production. I left them running so you can watch the idea work.
ledger.title: A balance that's never redeemed twice
ledger.desc: The points balance is a ledger of expiring batches, not a counter. FIFO consumption (oldest first) and full traceability. Under concurrency, a database-level pessimistic lock ended the double redemptions.
sqs.title: The queue that delivers 100%
sqs.desc: Twilio's rate limit was dropping notifications. A FIFO queue on AWS SQS serializes sends: transient failures go back to the queue, permanent ones are discarded and, once retries run out, the message lands in a DLQ with controlled redrive.
ga.title: A timetable that solves itself
ga.desc: The project that earned me honors student at IFTM: timetable generation with genetic algorithms. This miniature evolves live until conflicts hit zero — a teacher in two classes at the same time.
wins.honor.desc: IFTM · timetabling project with genetic algorithms
exp.title: Journey
exp.gd.desc: Backend to mobile: the loyalty system (ledger), the SQS queues, facial validation and A/B tests that lifted signup conversion by 12%.
exp.bw.desc: IFTM's junior enterprise. I led the technical team on client projects — architecture, code and mentoring the newcomers.
exp.edu.desc: Research in optimization with genetic algorithms, and hackathon wins on the record.
contact.title: Shall we build something that doesn't fall over?
contact.body: Backend, agtech, a hairy concurrency problem or just a good idea. Drop me a line — it'll be a pleasure.
footer.built: Handmade with HTML, CSS and JS. No framework.
